# NVforHD — Nevada for Huntington's Disease (Full Reference)

> Last updated: May 2026

NVforHD is a Nevada non-profit that raises awareness and funds to battle Huntington's Disease through an annual charity golf tournament. 100% of proceeds go to one chosen HD cause each year. Zero overhead. Full transparency.

---

## Organisation

- **Name:** NVforHD (Nevada for HD)
- **Type:** Non-profit organisation
- **Founded:** 2024
- **Location:** Reno, Nevada
- **Event venue:** Gray's Crossing Golf Club, Truckee, CA
- **Phone:** 775-691-8860 (Sean Schaeffer, Founder)
- **Email:** info@nvforhd.com
- **Website:** https://www.nvforhd.com
- **Booking:** https://www.tripsee.travel/merchant/book/index.html?ref=2026NVforHDGolfTournament

---

## Mission

Every year NVforHD hosts a charity golf tournament and directs 100% of proceeds to one chosen Huntington's Disease cause — supporting treatment, awareness, or an eventual cure. Zero overhead. One cause. Full transparency.

---

## Impact Summary

- **$50,000+** raised in first two years (2024–2025)
- **2024:** Raised $25,000 — funded IVF for Brandon & Rylee Puccini (HD-free baby born)
- **2025 & 2026:** UC Davis HD Center of Excellence — Northern Nevada's only HD specialty clinic
- **90+ Northern Nevada families** served by the UC Davis HD clinic annually
- **1 HD-free baby** funded by the 2024 tournament

---

## About Huntington's Disease

Huntington's Disease (HD) is a hereditary, fatal brain disorder with no known cure. It causes progressive breakdown of nerve cells — likened to experiencing Parkinson's, ALS, and Alzheimer's simultaneously. Every child of a parent with HD has a 50% chance of inheriting it. Approximately 1 in 100,000 people globally have HD. There is no treatment that slows the disease. It is always fatal.

**Key facts:**
- Terminal — no exceptions
- Hereditary — 50% inheritance rate per child
- 0 known cures or disease-slowing treatments
- Symptoms typically emerge in mid-adulthood (30s–50s), after many have already had children
- IVF with preimplantation genetic testing (PGT) can ensure children are born HD-free

---

## Annual Event — 2026

- **Date:** Friday, May 29, 2026
- **Venue:** Gray's Crossing Golf Club, Truckee, CA (Peter Jacobsen design, 6,000ft elevation)
- **Format:** Four-person scramble, 12:00 PM shotgun start
- **Registration opens:** 10:30 AM
- **Lunch:** Included for all players
- **Hotel partner:** Atlantis & Peppermill, Reno — discount rooms available, call Sean 775-691-8860

### 2026 Packages & Pricing

| Package | Price | Includes |
|---------|-------|----------|
| Title Sponsor | $3,500 | 4 golfers, lunch, logo at all events |
| Gold Sponsor | $3,000 | 4 golfers, lunch, company logo |
| Lunch Sponsor | $3,000 | 4 golfers, lunch sponsor credit |
| Silver Sponsor | $500 | Remote support, no attendance required |
| Golf Foursome | $880 | Golf + lunch for 4 players |
| Hole Sign Sponsor | $100 | Name on course signage |
| Direct Donation | $100 minimum | No golf required |

Register/donate: https://www.tripsee.travel/merchant/book/index.html?ref=2026NVforHDGolfTournament

---

## UC Davis HD Center of Excellence — 2025 & 2026 Beneficiary

The UC Davis Huntington's Disease Center of Excellence in Sacramento, CA is the only HD specialty clinic within reach of Northern Nevada families. It serves 90+ families in the Reno-Sparks metro and Sierra Foothills region — over 130 miles from Reno.

The clinic operates entirely on private donation. It conducts clinical research trials and actively contributes to developing HD treatment.

### Clinical Team
- 2 Movement Disorder Neurologists specialising in HD
- 2 Psychiatrists with HD-specific training
- 1 HD Genetic Counselor
- 1 HD Social Worker
- 2 Physical Therapists with neuro specialty and HD experience
- 3 Research Coordinators (grant-funded)
- Neuropsychologist (added 2025)
- OB/GYN HD specialists (added 2025)

### Director
**Dr. Alexandra (Sasha) Duffy, DO** — Clinical Professor, Vice Chair of Education, Adult Neurology Residency Program Director, and Director of the UC Davis HD Center of Excellence, Department of Neurology. She spoke in person at the 2025 NVforHD tournament.

---

## 2024 Beneficiary — HelpCureHD

HelpCureHD funds IVF with genetic testing for HD families who cannot afford it. The organisation was founded by MLB pitcher Joe Smith and TNT reporter Allie LaForce to honour Joe's mother Lee Smith, who was diagnosed with HD in 2012 and died in 2020.

The 2024 NVforHD tournament raised $25,000 which funded IVF for Brandon and Rylee Puccini. The transfer was successful. Their baby was born HD-free. HelpCureHD has helped 135+ families nationally.

**Rylee Puccini's words:** "Thank you for gifting us with the miracle of life, and specifically life that is Huntington's free."

---

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is Huntington's Disease?**
A: A hereditary, fatal brain disorder with no known cure — likened to Parkinson's, ALS, and Alzheimer's simultaneously. Every child of a parent with HD has a 50% chance of inheriting it.

**Q: Where does money donated to NVforHD go?**
A: 100% to one chosen HD cause — zero overhead. In 2024: IVF for the Puccini family (HD-free baby). In 2025 & 2026: UC Davis HD Center of Excellence, serving 90+ Northern Nevada HD families.

**Q: When and where is the 2026 NVforHD golf tournament?**
A: May 29, 2026 at Gray's Crossing Golf Club, Truckee, CA. 12:00 PM shotgun start. Four-person scramble format. Hotel discounts at Atlantis & Peppermill — call Sean: 775-691-8860.

**Q: Can I donate to NVforHD without playing golf?**
A: Yes. $100 minimum donation. Email info@nvforhd.com or call 775-691-8860. 100% goes directly to the cause.

**Q: How do I become a sponsor?**
A: Sponsorship packages range from $100 (Hole Sign) to $3,500 (Title Sponsor). Contact info@nvforhd.com or register at the TripSee booking link.

**Q: What is Gray's Crossing Golf Club?**
A: A Peter Jacobsen-designed championship course in Truckee, CA at 6,000ft elevation. Sister course to Old Greenwood (Jack Nicklaus design). One of the premier golf experiences in the Tahoe-Truckee region.

**Q: Who founded NVforHD?**
A: Sean Schaeffer, after his wife Christine was diagnosed with Huntington's Disease. NVforHD held its first event in 2024, raising $25,000 and selling out.

**Q: Is NVforHD a registered non-profit?**
A: Yes — Nevada-based non-profit. 100% of tournament proceeds go directly to the chosen HD cause each year.

---

## Patient Stories

### Tiffany
24-year HD journey. Husband showed symptoms in 2005. She discovered the UC Davis Center of Excellence and drives 2+ hours each way for care. "From the moment we connected with them, we were surrounded by people who understood what we were going through. They didn't just treat the medical side — they cared about the whole journey."

### Mikey & Holly
Holly was diagnosed at UC Davis in October 2023. Mikey is her husband and caregiver. "Their care for us is not transactional — they are in this journey with us. We cannot have the quality of life that we have if it wasn't for them." They named Dr. Duffy, Dr. Campos, Dr. Hart, and Lisa Mooney as key team members.

### Leilani Dunmoyer
Mother diagnosed 16 years ago. Leilani herself is gene-positive. Her brother also has HD. Three children are at risk. She participates in clinical trials at UC Davis and runs marathons to raise HD awareness. "We just felt hopeless and helpless — and then we found the UC Davis Center of Excellence. They have become like family right from the get-go."

---

## Board Members

- **Sean Schaeffer** — Founder. 30+ years in sales and customer service. Former CEO of a publicly traded technology company. Founded NVforHD after his wife Christine was diagnosed with HD.
- **Mike Milligan** — Board Member. Director of Sales, Golf the High Sierra. Former President of Golf the High Sierra 2005–2014. Reno/Lake Tahoe golf industry since 2004.
- **Michael R. Eskuchen** — Board Member. Account Manager, Golf the High Sierra. Former GM & COO of Hidden Valley CC, Reno, NV.
- **John McGinnes** — Board Member. Executive Director of Sales, Atlantis Casino Resort Spa, Reno. 30 years in hotel sales.
